text:
There was an unusual old man who lived by himself on a farm. He had odd habits; he wore a suit every day, even on Sundays, knitted his own socks, and spoke in rhymes. One day someone noticed how every time he stepped on the dewy morning lawn it'd skip.

"What on earth?" someone asked.

"Just an odd fancy," he replied mysteriously. "I've always found amusement in the unusual."

A year passed, and people began visiting him just to hear those whimsical narratives. One woman paused as a frog leaped into his hat. "How charming!"

"A tadpole!" he exclaimed. "Ah! That's my favorite..."

Frog vanished.

"But, frog!" he continued. "Tell me a delightful tale!"

He hummed a tune, spinning a yarn about a peculiar ant colony humming drones that sang about the adventure of the golden fleece. The frogs loved it. The man liked being weird.

One autumn there was a frosty night,
